title: agent.yaml — specialist agent identity + routing
description: Schema for agents/<name>/agent.yaml. Mapped field-by-field from agents.manifest.json, subagent.template.json, and agent-design-spec.md. See agent-folder.spec.md.

slug.description: Agent slug = folder name. Becomes @@AGENT_SLUG@@ (workflow name, file, node name) and the manifest intent.

intent.description: Classifier intent the orchestrator dispatches on (routed agents only; usually == slug).

description.description: The manifest registry description / routing hint. Keep it sharp.

confidence_threshold.description: Router dispatch threshold (routed agents). 0.7 normal, 0.0 for the fallback.

fallback.description: true only for the single fallback agent (unknown); omit otherwise.

model.description: @@MODEL@@. Default openrouter/auto; a specific model only with a reason.

architecture.description: From the design-spec. Default single-agent (no tools); single-llm = trivial one-shot, no tools.

reply_node_name.description: Optional display name of the LLM node. Default '<Slug> Reply' (code→Code Reply); set explicitly where it differs (ops→Ops Agent, unknown→Chat Agent).

memory.description: Optional. 'postgres' injects the memoryPostgresChat 'Conversation Memory' node (sessionKey tg:@@CHAT_ID@@) + ai_memory edge. Omit for a stateless agent.

reply_expression.description: Optional override for the Format Reply value. Default is the template's '={{ $json.text || $json.output }}'; agent-type agents that prioritise .output set '={{ $json.output || $json.text }}'.
